skill.name: constitution-guardian
skill.description: Real-time CC-DevFlow Constitution guardrail for detecting partial implementation notes and hardcoded secret patterns in development documents.
skill.summary: Checks devflow documents for Constitution violations before edits are saved.
skill.author: Dimon94

security_audit.summary: Static analysis flagged many backticks, secret strings, .env references, and markdown filenames in SKILL.md, but these are documentation examples and regex patterns rather than executable code or credential access. No prompt injection, network exfiltration, file reading, or real shell execution was found. The only confirmed concern is that the documented guardrail includes bypass controls, so publishers should describe that limitation clearly.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Markdown Backticks Flagged as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: The static analyzer reported Ruby or shell backtick execution, but the flagged text is Markdown formatting, fenced examples, inline filenames, regex examples, and configuration snippets. SKILL.md does not contain Ruby source, shell commands that execute, or an implementation that invokes external processes.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The complete file is documentation in Markdown, and the flagged backtick locations are display formatting or examples. I found no executable script or command invocation path.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Example Secret Patterns Are Not Credential Access
low_findings.description: The API key, token, password, and secret strings are intentionally shown as blocked examples or regex detection patterns. They are not read from environment variables, sent to a network endpoint, or used as live credentials.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The surrounding text says these values should be blocked or detected. There is no evidence of process environment reads, credential storage, or exfiltration logic.

low_findings.title: False Positive: Weak Cryptography Pattern Not Present
low_findings.description: The static blocker appears to have matched ordinary Markdown metadata, document filenames, JSON description text, and a design heading. I did not find MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or any cryptographic API usage in SKILL.md.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review of the cited lines found descriptive text and Markdown references only. No weak algorithm name or crypto operation appears in context.

low_findings.title: False Positive: .env References Are Remediation Guidance
low_findings.description: The .env references tell users to move secrets out of committed documents and into environment-based configuration. SKILL.md does not include code that opens, reads, writes, or uploads an environment file.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are remediation instructions and required practices. There is no filesystem access operation or implementation code around them.

low_findings.title: Documented Bypass Controls Reduce Enforcement Strength
low_findings.description: The skill documents bypass mechanisms through a marker, session flag, and SKIP_CONSTITUTION_CHECK environment override. These controls are not malicious, but they can weaken the guardrail if teams use them without review.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The bypass behavior is explicitly documented in multiple sections. It is a legitimate operational feature, but it creates a real governance risk if unmanaged.

content.value_statement: Devflow documents can drift into placeholders, deferred work, or unsafe secret examples. This skill defines guardrails that flag those violations during document edits.
actual_capabilities: Defines real-time checks for PRD, EPIC, TASKS, TECH_DESIGN, contract, and data model documents.
Flags TODO, FIXME, placeholder, simplified-for-now, and version deferral language.
Flags hardcoded API key, token, password, secret, and apiKey patterns in document content.
Describes blocking messages with line numbers and remediation steps for users.
Documents skip mechanisms for sessions, file markers, and environment overrides.
limitations: It covers only Articles I.1 and III.1, not the full Constitution.
It requires host hook configuration before it can block file edits.
Pattern matching can produce false positives or miss unusual wording.
Bypass controls can disable checks when teams permit them.

use_cases.title: Review requirement documents before edits are saved
use_cases.description: Detect placeholder text and deferred work in devflow documents before it becomes accepted planning material.

use_cases.title: Prevent unsafe secret examples in design documents
use_cases.description: Identify API key, password, token, and secret patterns inside documentation and code snippets.

use_cases.title: Configure guardrails for AI-assisted delivery workflows
use_cases.description: Use the documented rules to align Claude, Codex, and Claude Code workflows with CC-DevFlow standards.

prompt_templates.title: Check a devflow document
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner review of a single document
prompt_templates.prompt: Use constitution-guardian to review this devflow document for partial implementation language and hardcoded secret patterns. Report each issue with a plain remediation step.

prompt_templates.title: Rewrite blocked planning text
prompt_templates.prompt: Use constitution-guardian rules to rewrite this PRD section so it contains complete requirements and no TODO, FIXME, placeholder, or deferred-version language.

prompt_templates.title: Prepare a team guardrail checklist
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a short checklist for applying constitution-guardian to PRD, EPIC, TASKS, TECH_DESIGN, contract, and data model files.

prompt_templates.title: Audit a workflow for bypass risk
prompt_templates.prompt: Evaluate how constitution-guardian skip markers, session skips, and environment overrides could affect our review process. Recommend controls for approved exceptions.

output_examples.input: A TASKS document contains TODO later and a hardcoded password example.
output_examples.output: The review flags two violations: incomplete implementation language and a hardcoded secret pattern. It recommends replacing the TODO with complete acceptance criteria and moving the password to approved configuration guidance.

output_examples.input: A TECH_DESIGN document says a feature will be completed in version two.
output_examples.output: The review identifies version deferral as a partial implementation risk. It suggests either defining the complete design now or removing the deferred scope from the committed document.

output_examples.input: A legacy PRD includes an approved exception marker.
output_examples.output: The review notes that the marker can bypass enforcement. It recommends recording the approval reason and scheduling a later compliance review.

best_practices: Use the guardrail with the full Constitution validator at phase completion.
Require a review reason for every approved bypass marker or environment override.
Keep secret examples generic and avoid realistic key or password values.
anti_patterns: Using bypass markers to avoid completing requirements work.
Treating pattern checks as a complete security review.
Committing realistic-looking secrets as documentation examples.

faq.question: Does this skill execute security scans by itself?
faq.answer: No. The skill documents guardrail rules and expected hook behavior. Enforcement requires compatible host configuration.

faq.question: Which Constitution articles does it cover?
faq.answer: It focuses on Article I.1 for complete implementation and Article III.1 for hardcoded secrets.

faq.question: Can it replace a full Constitution validation script?
faq.answer: No. It is designed for real-time checks on critical rules. Full validation still needs the broader validator.

faq.question: Will it catch every secret?
faq.answer: No. It uses pattern matching, so unusual formats may be missed and harmless examples may be flagged.

faq.question: Why are bypass controls included?
faq.answer: They support legacy migrations and approved exceptions. Teams should track and review each bypass use.

faq.question: Which AI tools are listed as compatible?
faq.answer: The report lists Claude, Codex, and Claude Code as supported tools for marketplace discovery.
